title: Sean Penn Wins Best Supporting Actor at 2026 Oscars
text: Sean Penn claimed the Best Supporting Actor award at the 98th Academy Awards for his role as Col. Steven J. Lockjaw in One Battle After Another. This marks his third Oscar win and sixth nomination overall. Penn competed against Benicio Del Toro, Jacob Elordi, Delroy Lindo, and Stellan Skarsgård for the honor. Since the actor did not attend the ceremony, last year's winner Kieran Culkin accepted on his behalf, quipping that Sean Penn couldn't be here, or didn't want to. Penn's previous Oscar wins came for Mystic River (2004) and Milk (2009). He had been nominated three times prior for performances in Dead Man Walking, Sweet and Lowdown, and I Am Sam, but had not won until his first victory over two decades ago.
